libraryDescription:
This library, graphics3d.Graphics3DFramework, is a lightweight framework for handling 3D computer graphics easily.

When writing a 3D CG program from scratch using only the standard VCSSL libraries Graphics3D and GUI,
you need to write several fixed pieces of code every time, such as creating the window and renderer, the animation loop,and basic event handlers.
Graphics3DFramework supports easy 3D CG development by preparing such fixed processing in advance as a framework template.

Required Processing

Structurally, Graphics3DFramework is already a complete 3D CG program by itself, and it can actually run as a standalone program.
However, by itself no 3D models are placed, so only a white screen is displayed.

In practice, you import Graphics3DFramework from your own module and describe the required processing, such as placing and moving 3D models.
Such processing is written by defining functions with specific names, just like event handlers for GUI components.
Then Graphics3DFramework automatically executes those event handler functions at appropriate timings.

Graphics3DFramework automatically executes the following event handler functions. Their definitions are optional, so define only the ones you need.

  • void onStart( int renderer ) - Executed only once at startup. Write initialization processing such as creating and placing 3D models here. The old name initialize is also supported.
  • void onExit( int renderer ) - Executed only once at shutdown. Write any shutdown processing here if needed. The old name finalize is also supported.
  • void onPaint( int renderer ) - Executed every time the screen is updated, usually dozens of times per second. Write processing here when you want to draw foreground or background 2D CG contents. The old name paint is also supported.
  • void onUpdate( int renderer ) - Executed every time between screen updates, usually dozens of times per second. Write processing here when you want to move 3D models and so on. The old name update is also supported.
  • void onResize( int renderer ) - Executed when the window size changes. Write processing here when you want to adjust the layout of GUI components placed on the screen. The old name resize is also supported.
In all of the above functions, the argument renderer receives the ID of the 3D CG renderer. Use it for placing 3D models and related processing.

Background and Foreground 2D CG Drawing

Graphics3DFramework also supports foreground and background 2D CG drawing.
However, this feature is disabled by default, so before using it, callsetForegroundRendererState ( true ) andsetBackgroundRendererState ( true ) respectively.
After that, obtain the IDs of the 2D CG renderers withgetForegroundRenderer ( ) andgetBackgroundRenderer ( ), and perform drawing in functions such as onPaint.

Window Customization

Graphics3DFramework allows margin areas to be set inside the window, where GUI components such as buttons and text fields can be placed.
Margins can be configured with functions such as setMarginTop ( int margin ) and setMarginLeft ( int margin ).
Then obtain the window ID by getWindow ( ) and place GUI components freely in the margin areas.

Frame Rate Adjustment

Graphics3DFramework also has an automatic frame rate adjustment mechanism.
Specifically, it measures the time required for each frame and automatically adjusts the wait time of the animation loop so that the measured frame rate approaches the configured target frame rate.
Set the target frame rate with setFrameRate ( float rate ).The measured frame rate can be obtained with getCurrentFrameRate ( ).

Slowdown

Depending on the processing speed of the execution environment, if the drawing area is large or the drawing contents are heavy,
the measured frame rate may fail to reach the target frame rate even when the animation wait time is minimized. This phenomenon is a slowdown.

When a slowdown occurs, screen repainting may fail to keep up and the display may flicker.
In that case, flicker can be reduced by setting the target frame rate to a value with enough margin below the measured frame rate limit.
However, because the measured frame rate depends heavily on the environment,flicker can also be reduced by setting a relatively large lower limit for the animation wait time in milliseconds with setAnimationWaitMin ( int wait ).
Note that this lowers the upper limit of the measured frame rate.

function0.name: getGraphics
function0.description: Returns the graphics resource ID of the 3D CG graphics.
function0.returnValue: (int type) The graphics resource ID of the 3D CG graphics.

function1.name: getBackgroundGraphics
function1.description: Returns the graphics resource ID of the background 2D CG graphics.
function1.returnValue: (int type) The graphics resource ID of the background 2D CG graphics.

function2.name: getForegroundGraphics
function2.description: Returns the graphics resource ID of the foreground 2D CG graphics.
function2.returnValue: (int type) The graphics resource ID of the foreground 2D CG graphics.

function3.name: getRenderer
function3.description: Returns the renderer ID of the 3D CG renderer.
function3.returnValue: (int type) The renderer ID of the 3D CG renderer.

function4.name: getBackgroundRenderer
function4.description: Returns the renderer ID of the background 2D CG renderer.
function4.returnValue: (int type) The renderer ID of the background 2D CG renderer.

function5.name: getForegroundRenderer
function5.description: Returns the renderer ID of the foreground 2D CG renderer.
function5.returnValue: (int type) The renderer ID of the foreground 2D CG renderer.

function6.name: setBackgroundRendererState
function6.description: Sets whether the background 2D CG renderer is enabled.
function6.parameters: (bool type) state : Whether the background 2D CG renderer is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function7.name: setForegroundRendererState
function7.description: Sets whether the foreground 2D CG renderer is enabled.
function7.parameters: (bool type) state : Whether the foreground 2D CG renderer is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function8.name: getImageLabel
function8.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the image label displaying the drawing contents.
function8.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the image label.

function9.name: getGraphicsLabel
function9.description: Returns the GUI component ID of the image label.The newer getImageLabel function is available for this purpose.This function is supported for compatibility.
function9.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the image label.

function10.name: getWindow
function10.description: Gets the GUI component ID of the window.
function10.returnValue: (int type) The GUI component ID of the window.

function11.name: setAnimationState
function11.description: Sets whether animation mode is enabled.
function11.parameters: (bool type) enable : Whether animation mode is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function12.name: getAnimationState
function12.description: Gets whether animation mode is enabled.
function12.returnValue: (bool type) Whether animation mode is enabled. It is enabled when true.

function13.name: setFrameRate
function13.description: Sets the screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function13.parameters: (float type) rate : The screen update frequency.

function14.name: getFrameRate
function14.description: Gets the configured screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function14.returnValue: (float type) The configured screen update frequency.

function15.name: getCurrentFrameRate
function15.description: Gets the measured screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function15.returnValue: (float type) The measured screen update frequency.

function16.name: getLastFrameTime
function16.description: Gets the time of the last screen update of the animation.The returned time usually represents the elapsed time from program startup in milliseconds.However, the unit may differ depending on the implementation.To eliminate implementation dependence completely, convert it through the Time.millisecond function before using it.
function16.returnValue: (int type) The time of the last screen update, usually in milliseconds.

function17.name: getCurrentFrameRate
function17.description: Gets the measured screen update frequency of the animation, that is, the number of redraws per second.
function17.returnValue: (float type) The measured screen update frequency.

function18.name: getLastFrameTime
function18.description: Gets the time of the last screen update of the animation.The returned time usually represents the elapsed time from program startup in milliseconds.However, the unit may differ depending on the implementation.To eliminate implementation dependence completely, convert it through the Time.millisecond function before using it.
function18.returnValue: (int type) The time of the last screen update, usually in milliseconds.

function19.name: setPaintGraphics2DState
function19.description: Sets whether Graphics3D.paintGraphics2D is called automatically for background and foreground drawing when the screen is drawn, before the paint call.If true is specified, it is called automatically.If false is set, it is not called automatically, so it needs to be called explicitly in the paint event handler or elsewhere.
function19.parameters: (bool type) state : The state to set.

function20.name: getPaintGraphics3DState
function20.description: Gets whether Graphics3D.paintGraphics3D is called automatically when the screen is drawn, before the paint call.

function21.name: setMagnification
function21.description: Sets the drawing scale. The scale corresponds to the radius in pixels at which a circle of radius 1.0 located at a distance of 1.0 from the viewpoint is drawn.

function22.name: getMagnification
function22.description: Gets the drawing scale. The scale corresponds to the radius in pixels at which a circle of radius 1.0 located at a distance of 1.0 from the viewpoint is drawn.

function23.name: setBackgroundColor
function23.declaration: void setBackgroundColor(int r, int g, int b, int a)
function23.description: Sets the background color. Specify the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function23.parameters:
(int type) r : The red component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) g : The green component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) b : The blue component of the background color, from 0 to 255. 
(int type) a : The alpha component of the background color, from 0 to 255.

function24.name: setBackgroundColor
function24.description: Sets the background color. Specify an array storing the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function24.parameters: (int[] type) color : The background color array, where [0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, and [3]=alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function25.name: getBackgroundColor
function25.description: Gets the background color. The returned value is an array storing the red, green, blue, and alpha components of the background color in the range from 0 to 255.
function25.returnValue: (int[] type) The background color array, where [0]=red, [1]=green, [2]=blue, and [3]=alpha, each in the range from 0 to 255.

function26.name: setDirectionalLightVector
function26.declaration: void setDirectionalLightVector(float x, float y, float z)
function26.description: Sets the direction vector of the directional light source. Specify the vector pointing toward the direction in which the light exists.
function26.parameters:
(float type) x : The X component. 
(float type) y : The Y component. 
(float type) z : The Z component.

function27.name: setDirectionalLightVector
function27.description: Sets the direction vector of the directional light source. Specify the vector pointing toward the direction in which the light exists.
function27.parameters: (float[] type) v : The array of vector components, where v[0]=X, v[1]=Y, and v[2]=Z.

function28.name: getDirectionalLightVector
function28.description: Gets the direction vector of the directional light source. The returned vector points toward the direction in which the light exists.
function28.returnValue: (float[] type) The array of vector components, where v[0]=X, v[1]=Y, and v[2]=Z.

function29.name: setDirectionalLightBrightness
function29.description: Sets the intensity of the directional light source.

function30.name: getDirectionalLightBrightness
function30.description: Gets the intensity of the directional light source.

function31.name: setAmbientLightBrightness
function31.description: Sets the intensity of the ambient light source.

function32.name: getAmbientLightBrightness
function32.description: Gets the intensity of the ambient light source.

function35.name: setWindowSize
function35.parameters:
(int type) width : The width. 
(int type) height : The height.

function36.name: setWindowSize
function36.parameters: (int[] type) size : The window size, where [0]=width and [1]=height.

function37.name: getWindowSize
function37.returnValue: (int[] type) The window size, where [0]=width and [1]=height.

function38.name: setWindowLocation
function38.parameters:
(int type) x : The X coordinate of the top-left corner of the window. 
(int type) y : The Y coordinate of the top-left corner of the window.

function39.name: setWindowLocation
function39.parameters: size : The coordinates of the top-left corner of the window, where [0]=X and [1]=Y.

function40.name: getWindowLocation
function40.returnValue: (int[] type) The coordinates of the top-left corner of the window, where [0]=X and [1]=Y.

function41.name: setMarginRight
function41.description: Sets the right margin of the window.
function41.parameters: (int type) margin : The margin in pixels.

function42.name: getMarginRight
function42.description: Gets the right margin of the window.

function43.name: setMarginLeft
function43.description: Sets the left margin of the window.

function44.name: getMarginLeft
function44.description: Gets the left margin of the window.

function45.name: setMarginTop
function45.description: Sets the top margin of the window.

function46.name: getMarginTop
function46.description: Gets the top margin of the window.

function47.name: setMarginBottom
function47.description: Sets the bottom margin of the window.

function48.name: getMarginBottom
function48.description: Gets the bottom margin of the window.

function49.name: setAnimationWaitMin
function49.description: Sets the lower limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function50.name: getAnimationWaitMin
function50.description: Sets the lower limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function51.name: setAnimationWaitMax
function51.description: Sets the upper limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function52.name: getAnimationWaitMax
function52.description: Sets the upper limit of the animation wait time, that is, the wait time of the main loop.

function53.name: setMainLoopState
function53.description: Sets whether the main loop continues. If it is set to false, the animation main loop exits and the program ends.
function53.parameters: (bool type) state : Whether the main loop continues.

function54.name: getMainLoopState
function54.description: Gets whether the main loop continues.
function54.returnValue: (bool type) Whether the main loop continues.

function55.name: getMainArgument
function55.description: Gets the arguments of the main function.
function55.returnValue: (string[] type) The arguments of the main function.

function56.name: export
function56.declaration: void export( string filePath, string format, float quality )
function56.description: Outputs the current contents of the drawing label as an image file.File output is not performed at the moment this function is called.It is executed at the next screen update timing.
function56.parameters:
(string type) filePath : The output file name or file path. 
(string type) format : The file format, either "PNG" or "JPEG". 
(float type) quality : The quality.

function57.name: main
function57.description: The main function. It is normally executed automatically and calls the frameworkMain function.However, if the main program defines the main function,this function is no longer executed automatically, sothe frameworkMain function below needs to be called explicitly.
function57.parameters: (string[] type) args : The main arguments, which can also be obtained with the getMainArgument function.

function58.name: frameworkMain
function58.description: The main processing of the framework. It is normally executed automatically from the main function.However, if the main program defines the main function,it is no longer executed automatically, so it needs to be called at an appropriate timing.
function58.parameters: (string[] type) args : The main arguments, which can also be obtained with the getMainArgument function.

function59.name: onWindowClose
function59.description: The event handler called when the window is closed.
function59.parameters: (int type) id : The GUI component ID of the window.

function60.name: onWindowResize
function60.declaration: void onWindowResize( int id, int width, int height )
function60.description: The event handler called when the window is resized.
function60.parameters:
(int type) id : The GUI component ID of the window. 
(int type) width : The width of the window. 
(int type) height : The height of the window.

function61.name: paintWindow
function61.description: Repaints the window.It is normally executed automatically, butIt is normally executed automatically, but when animation is disabled and in similar cases,it needs to be called explicitly when the screen is updated.

function62.name: render
function62.description: Calls the paint function and related processing to render the screen contents.It also composites the 3D CG contents with the background and foreground 2D CG contents.It is normally executed automatically, but when animation is disabled and in similar cases,it needs to be called explicitly when the screen is updated.
